---
title: "Syllabus Summary"
---

## Catalog Description

This course develops the mathematical foundations of imaging science from a modern inverse problems perspective. Students learn how images are formed, degraded, and reconstructed using tools from linear algebra, Fourier analysis, probability, variational modeling, and optimization.

The course emphasizes mathematical modeling, stability analysis, regularization techniques such as Tikhonov, Total Variation, and sparse methods, and computational implementation in Python.

## Main Text

M. Bertero and P. Boccacci, *Introduction to Inverse Problems in Imaging*, 2nd edition, CRC Press, 2021.

## References

- A. Chambolle and T. Pock, "An introduction to continuous optimization for imaging," *Acta Numerica*, 25, 161-319, 2016.
- S. Mallat, *A Wavelet Tour of Signal Processing*, 3rd edition, Academic Press, 2008.

## Assessment Structure

| Assessment | Weight | Timing |
|---|---:|---|
| Quizzes | 15% | Weeks 4 and 12 |
| Two-student modeling project | 30% | Assigned Week 6, report Week 14, defense Week 15 |
| Semester examination | 25% | Week 8 |
| Final examination | 30% | Exam week |
